creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Zane C. Bowers-Hadley from comment #27)
> Bad performance is better than being nonfunctional and FS issues.
Disabling UBLIO breaks every day usage (copying files).  Enabling it breaks creating alien filesystem.  I understand that it's a nuisance, but most users of this port need it to work with *existing* NTFS filesystems, not to create new ones.  Perhaps, while waiting for a real fix, we could prevent `mkntfs' from taking the buggy code path, I'll look into that when time permits.
